message:
test(cve_arena): refresh bd-1qy futex mutex artifacts via rch remote runner

Re-regenerate the full result set for the bd-1qy futex mutex CVE-arena
fixture under `tests/cve_arena/results/bd-1qy/`. All 10 scenarios
(5 strict + 5 hardened, covering rust_mutex_roundtrip_trylock_busy,
rust_mutex_contention_counters, rust_mutex_destroy_while_locked_ebusy,
rust_mutex_unlock_without_lock_eperm, and c_fixture_pthread_mutex_adversarial)
still pass with exit 0.

What actually changed in the artifacts:

- `report.json`: `generated_at` bumped from 2026-03-25 to 2026-04-11,
  and `timing_ns` reflects the new run (the Rust mutex scenarios now
  take ~34s wall because they are being executed remotely through
  `rch` against worker `ts2`, which includes dependency sync + remote
  compile + artifact retrieval; the C fixture stays at ~100ms because
  it does not require the rch round-trip).
- `artifact_index.json`: `generated_utc` timestamp and every per-file
  `sha256`/`size_bytes` entry regenerated to match the new stdout/stderr
  captures.
- Per-scenario `stdout.txt`: now empty for every Rust scenario because
  `cargo test --nocapture --test-threads=1` under rch serializes both
  the `running 1 test` banner and the `test result: ok.` line into
  stderr (interleaved with rch's own INFO-level tracing), so stdout
  has no residual content. The C fixture still writes its expected
  line to stdout.
- Per-scenario `stderr.txt`: now contains the rch pipeline trace
  (`Selected worker ts2`, per-crate `Syncing .../Sync completed`,
  `Executing command remotely: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test
  pthread_mutex_core_test ... -- --exact --nocapture --test-threads=1`,
  the `Finished test profile` line, the `running 1 test` / `test
  result: ok` output, and the `Artifacts retrieved` footer) followed
  by the `[RCH] remote ts2 (34.4s)` summary.
- `trace.jsonl`: regenerated with the new per-scenario timing samples.

No test logic, no fixture binaries, and no scenario definitions were
changed. This is purely a result-artifact refresh so the checked-in
CVE-arena baseline matches the current execution path (local cargo →
rch remote worker). Follows the same pattern as 5f06d51
("regenerate conformance/CVE arena artifacts").
